title: Isolation of Membrane-enriched Fractions from Mouse Cortical Neurons
author: Odetta Antico
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bxjupknw

steps.title: To depolarize or uncouple mitochondrial membrane...
steps.instruction: To depolarize or uncouple mitochondrial membrane potential in neurons, cultures could be until to 09:00:00 with a combination of 10 micromolar (µM) Antimycin A and 1 micromolar (µM) Oligomycin dissolved in DMSO at 37 °C .

steps.title: Gently aspirate media from wells and...
steps.instruction: Gently aspirate media from wells and add 1 mL of PBS+inhibitors in each well at Room temperature . Note : Do not use cold PBS, otherwise neuronal cells can detach from the dishes.

steps.title: Scrape neuronal cells and collect in...
steps.instruction: Scrape neuronal cells and collect in a 50 mL labelled Falcon tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge neuronal cells at 500 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge neuronal cells at 500 x g, °C, for 00:03:00 at 4 °C .

steps.title: Resuspend pellets in 2 mL of...
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellets in 2 mL of HB buffer + inhibitors freshly added.

steps.title: Homogenise cells using a stainless steel...
steps.instruction: Homogenise cells using a stainless steel Dounce homogeniser tissue grinder with 40 stroke. Note : It is recommended to slowly press and twist the pestle on the sample. When the pestle is raised and turned, a strong vacuum force is generated creating shearing forces that will help to generate a fine homogenate. During this action, do not completely remove the pestle, this to avoid the generation of bubbles and to retain cellular organelles intact. Check cell lysis with Trypan blue (4ul lysate + 4ul trypan blue), 90% of cells should be disrupted.

steps.title: Pellet nuclei and the remaining intact...
steps.instruction: Pellet nuclei and the remaining intact cells from lysate at 2000 rpm, °C, for 00:05:00 at 4 °C . The subsequent supernatant is the post-nuclear supernatant (PNS).

steps.title: For total membrane isolation, transfer the...
steps.instruction: For total membrane isolation, transfer the PNS to an ultracentrifuge tube. Note : Fill tube at least 4/5 with HB buffer to prevent tube collapse and equalize weight to <0.01g.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant, this represents the cytosolic fraction
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant, this represents the cytosolic fraction.

steps.title: Pellet represents the membrane fractions
steps.instruction: Pellet represents the membrane fractions. Freeze the pellet or resuspend the pellet according to the experiment.

steps.title: For Western Blotting or proteomic experiments
steps.instruction: For Western Blotting or proteomic experiments: transfer the pellet using a tip of a pipette to an Eppendorf tube and add 200 µL - 300 µL of MitoBuffer. Sonicate the membrane pellet with a probe sonicator 20% amplitude for 00:00:05 or until the pellet is completely resuspended.

steps.title: Proceed with protein quantification by using...
steps.instruction: Proceed with protein quantification by using the Coomassie Protein Assay and sample preparation according to the experiment.

steps.title: Membrane lysates can be stored at -80 °C
steps.instruction: Membrane lysates can be stored at -80 °C .
